Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results section explicitly states TaMPE-D/TaFAD8-D is TraesCS2D02G082700.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Figures/phylogeny and materials identify bread wheat as T. aestivum / Triticum aestivum.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: TaFAD8-D expression/promoter variation and mutant analyses involve TAM107, Chinese Spring, and Fielder.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Results involve TAM107, LZ238, Chinese Spring, Fielder, and NingChun 4/NC4 backgrounds.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Candidate gene is described as encoding fatty acid desaturase 8 / ω-3 fatty acid desaturase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper identifies TaFAD8-D as FAD8, an omega-3 fatty acid desaturase; plastid/chloroplast localization is shown.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results directly connect TaFAD8 to fatty acid biosynthesis and lipid remodelling under heat stress.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Subcellular localization to chloroplast membrane and effects on chloroplast/thylakoid lipids support plastid chloroplast membrane lipid desaturation branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Paper supports TaFAD8 acts in fatty-acid desaturation in chloroplast membranes, but does not explicitly label it as a 'downstream' step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results state TaFAD8 plays a key role in catalysing conversion of C18:2 to C18:3; substrate is C18:2 in membrane lipids.

Primary_Product.reason: Results support C18:3 as the product increased by TaFAD8 activity and reduced in mutants.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Directly supported by fatty acid data and text stating TaFAD8 catalyses conversion of C18:2 to C18:3 in chloroplast-localized context.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 2E shows TaFAD8-D-GFP exclusively localized on the chloroplast membrane.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure S3C/S3D and text state TaFAD8 is mainly expressed in leaf and stem, lower in spike/root, and downregulated during heat stress.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Figure 2B shows higher basal TaFAD8-D expression in CS/NIL-CS than TAM107/NIL-TAM107 and downregulation by HS in seedling leaves.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results repeatedly use CK 24°C, HS mainly 42°C, promoter assays at 37°C, and post-anthesis field heat stress.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: C18:3 is directly supported, and lipid composition/remodelling is supported, but calling remodelled chloroplast lipids a single 'terminal metabolite' is imprecise.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Measured outputs include fatty acids and multiple lipid classes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results link altered fatty acid/lipid composition to membrane fluidity/integrity, chloroplast/thylakoid stability, photosynthetic efficiency, and thermotolerance.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaf chloroplast/thylakoid-associated membranes are supported by localization and lipid analyses; grain fatty acids were also assessed.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Paper supports a direct catalytic role in C18:3 formation with downstream effects on lipid remodelling.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Higher TaFAD8 expression is associated with higher C18:3/lower C18:2:C18:3 ratio and poorer heat tolerance; mutants/promoter downregulation improve Fv/Fm, survival, and TGW under HS.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported in Results: fine mapping, promoter TE lowering expression, CRISPR improving HS traits, and effects on C18:2-to-C18:3/lipid remodelling/chloroplast and Ca2+ homeostasis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The qualifier 'not explicitly established as rate-limiting' is accurate; paper supports TaFAD8 as a key control point for C18:2/C18:3 balance under HS.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported in Results/Methods and used to validate TaFAD8 function.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Figure 2C/D and Figure 6 use native CS and TAM107 promoters plus a modified TAM107 promoter lacking TE insertion.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: TaWRKY71 is shown to bind the TaFAD8 promoter and regulate TaFAD8 transcription.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Field data show no significant differences in major agronomic traits under normal conditions, while TGW is improved under HS.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq, resequencing/BSA, fatty acid profiling, and lipidomics are all present; no accession numbers are provided in the paper, so 'NA' is acceptable.

Epigenetic_Regulation.reason: TE insertion in promoter suppressing transcription is supported, but classifying this as 'epigenetic regulation' is not explicitly shown; it is more clearly structural cis-regulatory variation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Results and Discussion support using Hap4 promoter allele or loss-of-function/editing alleles to improve thermotolerance, including introgression into NC4.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper supports three homoeologs, D-homoeolog promoter mapping, both ABD and D-only mutants improving heat tolerance, and Hap4 being rare in modern wheat and likely originating from Aegilops tauschii.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Bread wheat/Triticum aestivum is indicated in figures and materials.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: TaWRKY71 experiments and mutants are in the Fielder background/WT.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: TaWRKY71 functional assays are performed in Fielder WT and mutants.

Regulator_Type.reason: TaWRKY71 is explicitly treated as a transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: WRKY family identity and W-box binding are directly supported by phylogeny and EMSA.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 6B/text state TaWRKY71 transcript gradually increased during HS in leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Measured in leaves of 7-day-old seedlings and induced by HS.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Heat stress is the explicit inducing condition examined.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Knockout data in Figure 6G measure TaFAD8-A, TaFAD8-B, and TaFAD8-D, all increased in Tawrky71 mutants.

Regulation_Mode.reason: EMSA and dual-LUC support direct binding to W-box motif in TaFAD8 promoter and transcriptional repression.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Paper explicitly states knockout increases TaFAD8 expression under CK and HS.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Through TaFAD8 repression, TaWRKY71 is linked to C18:2/C18:3 balance and lipid metabolism/remodelling under HS.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Paper measures C18:2 and C18:3 and discusses fine-tuning their ratio; 'membrane lipid remodelling state' is broader than a metabolite.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Outputs affected are fatty acids and lipids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results link altered C18:2/C18:3 balance to membrane integrity, photosynthetic efficiency, and thermotolerance.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaf and chloroplast-associated context is plausible from TaFAD8 biology, but TaWRKY71 section itself only reports leaf fatty acids, not direct chloroplast-localized accumulation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Tawrky71 mutants had higher TaFAD8, lower C18:2/C18:3 ratio under HS, lower Fv/Fm, and lower survival under HS.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four listed findings are directly supported in Results section 2.6.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Paper states TaWRKY71 is an important repressor of TaFAD8 under HS and fine-tunes C18:2/C18:3 ratio and thermotolerance.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed validation methods are described and used for TaWRKY71 analyses.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The value lists 'TaFAD8 promoter W-box,' which is a DNA element, not a protein; protein-protein interactors are not reported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: HS at 42°C and promoter assay at 37°C are explicitly reported.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Dual-LUC assays use CS, TAM107, and TE-deleted TAM107 TaFAD8-D promoters.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper reports heat susceptibility upon knockout and does not report other tradeoffs.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq-based regulatory network/candidate identification is described; no accession numbers are provided.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Results indicate TaWRKY71 repression of TaFAD8 promotes heat tolerance, so avoiding knockout or maintaining repression is a supported breeding implication.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Dual-LUC data support that the TAM107 promoter TE insertion abolishes detectable TaWRKY71-mediated repression, linking cis-variation with altered TF regulation.

corrections.reason: The value lists 'TaFAD8 promoter W-box,' which is a DNA element, not a protein; protein-protein interactors are not reported.
